case_title: TRINIDAD ORTEGA VS. NO FAULT ASPHALT, INC et al

docket_entries.description: RESPONSE TO ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: ARBITRATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF ORTEGA, TRINIDAD
DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FILED BY PLAINTIFF ORTEGA, TRINIDAD
OPPOSITION TO PLTF'S MOTION TO VACATE AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS FILED BY DEFENDANT NO FAULT ASPHALT

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED. PLAINTIFF TRINIDAD ORTEGA'S MOTION TO VACATE DISMISSAL IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL HAS SHOWN THAT HIS FAILURE TO APPEAR WAS DUE TO MISTAKE AND NEGLECT AS A RESULT OF A CALENDARING ERROR. CCP 473(B). PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL SHALL PAY DEFENDANT $1360 IN REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES (CCP 473(B)) PAYABLE NO LATER THAN SEPT. 4, 2012. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976
NOTICE TO PLAINTIFF
ANSWER TO COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFENDANT NO FAULT ASPHALT

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Dismiss And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-02-2012 Off Calendar

Mtn To Vacate
Off Calendar Aug-24-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lauren B. Gelber, Esq. of L/O of Brian Larsen, Tel. 415.398.5000, for Plaintiff and moving party via court call;
Jin Im, Esq. of McDowell Shaw Colman Garcia, Tel. 925.210.1300.x3006, for Defendant and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified. PLAINTIFF TRINIDAD ORTEGA's Motion To Vacate Dismissal is Granted. Plaintiff's counsel has shown that his failure to appear was due to mistake and neglect as a result of a calendaring error. CCP 473(b). Plaintiff's counsel shall pay Defendant $1360 in reasonable attorney's fees (CCP 473(b)) payable no later than Sept. 4, 2012. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

CONT. ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-07-2012 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Philip Anthony Pereira, Esq., appearing for Defendant. No appearance for Plaintiff. Court ordered case dismissed with prejudice. Proceedings not reported. (610)
